title: Revamp Hero Widget
body: Hero widget should the the identity of the app. Its a standard hero widget and doesn't really relates to the app itself. The background is generic etc. I want to have a hero widget which is very premium and is useful and relates to the app
enhanced_description:
The user wants the Hero widget redesigned to feel premium and be closely tied to BabyBloom's identity as a baby care tracking app.
- Current state: The hero widget uses a generic/standard design with a generic background that doesn't reflect the app's purpose.
- Desired state: A premium-looking hero widget that visually communicates baby care tracking, feels unique to BabyBloom, and is useful (not just decorative).
- Affected component: The Hero widget in the main/home view (src/ directory).
- Acceptance criteria: Hero widget has a custom, app-relevant design; background relates to baby/parenting theme; the widget should feel premium and polished; it should display useful information relevant to the user (e.g. baby info, next feed, quick stats).
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Replaced the hero widget section (lines 518-556) with a premium redesigned version.

Approach:
- Replaced the flat gradient card with a multi-layered design featuring decorative bokeh circles, glassmorphism containers, and a 3-color gradient (primary → secondary → purple).
- Added time-based greeting (Good morning/afternoon/evening) with baby name as the primary heading.
- Created a frosted-glass age badge showing age + milestone stage inline.
- Preserved the milestone progress ring but placed it in a frosted container for visual polish.
- Added today's quick stats bar showing feeds count, diapers count, and sleep hours (or sleeping indicator) in frosted pill containers.
- Feed reminder now has its own styled row with visual urgency differentiation (red-tinted for overdue).
- All existing functionality preserved: clickable milestone ring, feed reminder logic, age emoji stages.

Trade-offs:
- Uses backdrop-filter for frosted glass effect which may not render on very old browsers, but gracefully degrades.
- Slightly taller widget due to added stats row, but provides much more useful at-a-glance information.
